hypothesis: Replacing NN-BAX’s random on-path sampling with an ensemble-uncertainty acquisition that queries, at each iteration, the model-predicted path image with maximal predictive variance in the perpendicular force will reduce the number of true simulator evaluations needed to achieve the same fmax convergence threshold and ≤1% error in the transition-state energy barrier by at least 40% on LJ38 and comparably high-dimensional DFT pathways.
